title: Navgrah Mantra
intro: Navgrah Mantra with meaning. Chant this supreme mantra to receive the grace of the Navgrah.
description:
Om Dum Durgayai Namaha
Om, I bow down to the Goddess Durga, who is the protector and remover of all suffering and difficulties.

Om Hanumate Namaha
Om, I bow down to Lord Hanuman, the remover of all obstacles and the symbol of strength and devotion.

Om Aim Saraswatyai Namaha
Om, I bow down to the Goddess Saraswati, who is the embodiment of knowledge, wisdom, and arts.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya
Om, I bow to the Lord who dwells in the hearts of all beings (Lord Vishnu/Krishna).

Om Namo Narayanaya
Om, salutations to Lord Narayana.

Om Hraam Hreem Hraum Sah Suryaya Namaha
Om, salutations to the Sun God, who is the source of all energy and light.

Brahma Murari Tripurantakari Bhanuh Shashi Bhumi-suto Budhashcha. Gurushcha Shukrah Shani Rahu Ketavah Sarve Grahah Shantikara Bhavantu.
May Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva; the Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu, and Ketu—may all these planets bestow peace upon us.

faq.q1: When should Navgrah Mantra be chanted?
faq.a1: Navgrah Mantra is considered auspicious when chanted in the morning or during an auspicious time.
faq.q2: How many times should Navgrah Mantra be chanted?
faq.a2: It is commonly chanted 11, 21, 51, or 108 times.
faq.q3: What are the benefits of chanting Navgrah Mantra?
faq.a3: According to Hindu tradition, chanting Navgrah Mantra is believed to attract the blessings of the nine planets and promote harmony and positive energy.
faq.q4: Can Navgrah Mantra be chanted daily?
faq.a4: Yes, Navgrah Mantra can be chanted daily with devotion.
faq.a5: Yes, anyone may chant Navgrah Mantra with faith and devotion.
